executiveSummary.summary: You're tackling a real problem with solid technical chops, but facing three major headwinds: notoriously difficult sales to restaurants, POS incumbents adding your features, and a 3-month value gap. The business could work with aggressive customer success and tight POS integration, but requires careful positioning and realistic growth expectations.

marketSize.tam: $660M - US independent restaurant inventory software market (660K independent restaurants × $1K annual software spend)
marketSize.sam: $158M - restaurants with >$1M annual revenue that could afford $199/mo (132K restaurants)
marketSize.som: $2.4M - realistic 3-year capture of 1K restaurants at $199/mo (0.75% of SAM)
marketSize.methodology: Based on National Restaurant Association data, filtered by revenue thresholds and software adoption rates

competitors.name: Toast
competitors.description: POS system adding inventory management features
competitors.strength: Already integrated into restaurant operations, massive user base
competitors.weakness: Inventory is not their core focus, basic feature set

competitors.name: Square for Restaurants
competitors.strength: Easy setup, familiar brand, competitive pricing

marketAnalysis.positioning: The only inventory system that learns your restaurant's patterns to prevent waste before it happens
marketAnalysis.timing: Post-COVID margin pressure makes waste reduction critical, but restaurants are also cutting software spend

valueProposition.coreProblem: Independent restaurants waste 4-10% of food inventory due to poor demand forecasting, costing $1,600-4,000 monthly for typical locations
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Independent restaurants with $1M+ annual revenue, 15+ employees, existing POS system, and current food waste problems
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Only solution that predicts demand using external data (weather, events) plus historical patterns to prevent waste before ordering
valueProposition.whyNow: Post-pandemic margin compression makes waste reduction existential, while POS data standardization enables better integrations

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Monthly SaaS subscriptions at $199/location with potential upsells for multi-location accounts and supplier integration fees
businessModel.pricingFramework: $199/mo justified by saving $1,600+ monthly in food waste - 8x ROI if product delivers 20% waste reduction

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $2,400 (assumes 6-month sales cycle, $20K annual sales cost per rep covering 100 restaurants)

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: At 10x scale (10K restaurants), need 20+ sales reps, dedicated customer success, and significant ML infrastructure investment

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Python/Django backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL, AWS/GCP for ML pipeline, standard REST APIs for POS integrations

buildVsBuy.rationale: Use existing solutions like Revel or custom APIs - don't reinvent authentication and data sync
Core differentiation requires custom models incorporating weather/events data
Stripe or similar - not core to value prop

technicalRisks: POS API rate limits and reliability
ML model accuracy with limited data
technicalFeasibility.estimatedMvpTimeline: 4-6 months for production-ready version with 3 POS integrations

risks.description: Toast and Square bundle inventory features for free, making standalone tools obsolete
risks.mitigation: Focus on advanced ML features they can't match, become best-in-class integration partner

risks.description: Restaurant sales cycles are 6-12 months with high churn due to business failures
risks.mitigation: Target established restaurants (3+ years), focus on quick wins in first 30 days

risks.description: 3-month data requirement creates value gap where customers churn before seeing benefits
risks.mitigation: Provide immediate value through manual insights, benchmark reports, and basic automation

risks.description: POS integration complexity could delay launch and increase support burden
risks.mitigation: Start with 1-2 most common POS systems, use webhook-based architecture for reliability

risks.description: High CAC and long sales cycles could exhaust runway before achieving sustainable growth
risks.mitigation: Focus on referral programs, target restaurant groups for faster expansion

week1to4: Interview 20 restaurant owners about current inventory pain points and software buying process
Audit Toast and Square inventory features - document exact gaps
Map integration requirements for top 3 POS systems
Define success metrics for current pilot restaurants
week5to8: Build Toast integration MVP for pilot customers
Create 30-day value demonstration (manual insights + basic automation)
Test waste reduction vs cost savings messaging with 10 prospects
Develop referral program for pilot customers
week9to12: Launch paid pilot with 3 existing test restaurants at $99/mo
Create sales playbook based on pilot learnings
Establish customer success process for onboarding

decisionAgenda.question: Should we integrate with POS systems or build standalone?

options.tradeoff: Gain: easier adoption, better data. Lose: technical complexity, dependency risk
Gain: full control, simpler tech. Lose: adoption friction, data quality issues
Gain: flexibility, gradual migration. Lose: split development focus, unclear positioning

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Deep POS integration. Restaurant adoption requires minimal friction, and data quality is essential for ML accuracy. Start with Toast (largest independent market share), then Square.

decisionAgenda.question: Should we position around waste reduction or cost savings?

options.tradeoff: Gain: sustainability angle, measurable impact. Lose: harder to quantify ROI
Gain: clear ROI calculation, easier sales. Lose: commoditized positioning
Gain: broader appeal. Lose: unfocused positioning, confused value prop

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Cost savings as primary metric with waste reduction as supporting benefit. Restaurants are profit-driven; lead with '20% reduction in food costs = $2K monthly savings' then add 'plus help the environment.'

decisionAgenda.question: How do we bridge the 3-month ML value gap?

options.tradeoff: Gain: immediate value, customer retention. Lose: not scalable, resource intensive
Gain: scalable content, immediate utility. Lose: not personalized, lower impact
Gain: tangible workflow improvement. Lose: not differentiated, limited impact

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Combine manual insights with industry benchmarks. Provide personalized waste audit in month 1, industry comparison in month 2, then transition to ML predictions in month 3.
